name: Integrity Checker
overview: Re-hashes every file in your managed folders and flags any whose contents no longer match what Shoko has stored, leaving mismatches as unrecognized for re-matching — the same outcome as a manual single-file rescan, run in bulk with a small dashboard to drive it.

releases.release_notes:
## What changed

- **Renamed the plugin's page from "Integrity Checker" to "Dashboard"** in Settings → Plugins, matching the naming convention used by other plugins (e.g. ShokoRelay) instead of redundantly repeating the plugin's own name.

## What's fixed

- **The folder picker no longer "resets" mid-run.** Reloading the dashboard while a
  scoped run was executing snapped the picker back to "everything selected" — it now
  mirrors the run's *actual* scope (matched against the live status) until that run
  finishes, instead of silently disagreeing with what's underneath it.

## What's fixed

- **Drop-source ("import") folders no longer show up in the Scope picker.** They're a
  transient staging area — files land there briefly before being relocated to their real
  home, so offering them for an integrity check didn't make sense (and could even race
  with an in-progress import). Folders that double as both a drop source *and* destination
  are still offered, since they also serve as a permanent home for files.
- **Added a loading spinner to the Scope picker.** Fetching the folder list can take a
  noticeable moment on larger libraries, and the plain "Loading managed folders…" text
  alone didn't make it obvious that something was happening.

## What's fixed

- **Folder picker wasn't working**: managed folders failed to load/select correctly because
  the API returned the folder ID as `ID`, which the dashboard's camelCase normalizer turned
  into `iD` instead of `id` — leaving every folder's ID undefined client-side. Renamed the
  field to `ManagedFolderID` (matching the existing `FileID`/`PreviousVideoID` convention)
  so it now camelizes correctly to `managedFolderID`, and the picker loads and selects as
  expected.

## What's new

- **Scope your checks**: the dashboard now has a folder picker so you can run an integrity check
  against just the managed folders you choose, instead of always walking your entire library.
  Leave everything selected for the previous full-library behavior, or narrow it down for a
  faster, more targeted run. The picker is backed by a new `GET /folders` endpoint, and the
  dashboard's summary now shows which folders a run was scoped to (including after a restart).
- Tightened up the dashboard copy — the header and results panel were both explaining the
  detach/re-match mechanism; that's now said once, where it's relevant.
